73598.subject:
How did Anthropic's AI find 10,000 software security issues in 30
 days?
73598.body:
The Atlas

Автор: GenAI Works


Автор: GenAI Works

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/how-did-anthropics-ai-find-10000-software-security-issues-v9tte?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3Buv92y22yRlqLs%2F%2F7Y0Xp3Q%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=0i3v32rRd9Dcg1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mpll2eg6~w5-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mpll2eg6-w5
  
Welcome back! This week, Anthropic's most capable AI model quietly exposed a flaw in how the entire software industry handles security. On another update, hidden Claude Code feature surfaced before its official announcement and then vanished. Anthropic’s new joiner Karpathy has a framework for making AI coding actually work, and it has hit 82,000 GitHub stars. And, DeepSeek just rewrote the pricing rules for enterprise AI. A lot moved this week.  In today's Atlas Newsletter: Anthropic's AI found 10,000 critical vulnerabilities in 30 days: Claude Mythos Preview and 50 partners exposed the part of cybersecurity that AI hasn't fixed yet. Claude Code hid something worth watching: A workflows tool for multi-agent orchestration appeared in the codebase, then disappeared. Anthropic pulled it via a server-side flag. That looks like a staged release, and next week we'll show you how to use it. The 4 principles that make Claude Code actually work: The CEO of Google Sundar Pichai says a solo developer with Claude can outcompete a 10-person Google team. Karpathy's CLAUDE.md, now at 82,000 GitHub stars, is the setup that makes that true. What happens when DeepSeek becomes 75% cheaper: DeepSeek made its price cut permanent, putting V4 Pro at $268 to run a full intelligence benchmark. GPT-5.5 costs 12 times more for the same test. The AI model race now has a different scoreboard. PS: Want to share your AI tool or company news with our 14M+ community? Get in touch. How did Anthropic's AI find 10,000 software security issues in 30 days? In less than a month, Anthropic’s AI and around 50 partners surfaced over 10,000 high-severity software security. Cloudflare found 2,000 bugs across its critical systems. Mozilla found 271 vulnerabilities in Firefox 150, more than ten times what it found in Firefox 148 using Claude Opus 4.6. Also, several partners reported that their bug-finding rate increased by a factor of ten or more. Can you guess how? They used Claude Mythos Preview. And the burden has shifted beyond discovery. It now sits in verification, disclosure, patching, and deployment.  That change creates a specific risk: the same capability accelerating defense can also compress the time and cost required for offense. Anthropic has not released Mythos-class models publicly, citing the need for stronger safeguards before general release. But similar capabilities

🧠 AI’s Crypto News Brief: Market sentiment has dipped into "Extreme Fear" despite landmark institutional breakthroughs. Strategic Bitcoin disclosures from SpaceX and major regulatory shifts from the White House define a high-stakes 24 hours for the global digital asset ecosystem. 🚀SpaceX Discloses 18,712 BTC Holdings in New SEC Filing In a major filing ahead of its anticipated IPO, SpaceX revealed it holds over 18,700 Bitcoin, surpassing Tesla’s current reserves. The disclosure positions the aerospace giant as one of the world's most significant corporate BTC treasury holders. Read full story 🚫Indonesia Blocks Polymarket Amid National Gambling Crackdown Indonesia’s Ministry of Communication and Digital has restricted access to the decentralized prediction platform Polymarket, classifying it as illegal online gambling. The move follows a viral contract speculating on the country’s presidency. See details 🏛️Trump Issues Executive Order to Streamline Crypto Banking Access A new executive order from Donald Trump directs federal regulators to review and open federal payment rails to compliant digital asset firms. The order mandates a 90-day review period to identify and remove barriers to institutional banking partnerships. Read full story 🌍Tether Partners with Georgia for GELT Stablecoin Launch Tether has announced the launch of "GELT," a stablecoin pegged to the Georgian lari, with full backing from the Georgian government. The project aims to integrate blockchain-based programmable payments into the nation’s core financial infrastructure. See details 🛡️Ethereum Upgrade "Hegota" to Feature Native Protocol Privacy Developers have proposed EIP-8182 for Ethereum’s upcoming Hegota upgrade, introducing native private transfers for ETH and ERC-20 tokens. The proposal seeks to solve liquidity fragmentation by integrating a shared protocol-managed shielded pool. Read full story 🤝SWIFT Directory Formally Recognizes Ripple Treasury Integration Ripple Treasury has been added to SWIFT’s Business Solutions Providers Directory as a certified partner for ISO 20022 compliance. This certification marks a pivotal moment for the institutional adoption of Ripple’s liquidity solutions within traditional banking. Read full story 📉Analysts Signal Market Rotation Despite Record Bitcoin ETF Outflows While U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $1.26 billion in

73590.subject: 🔷 The Daily: Vitalik Buterin says Ethereum Foundation will sell less ETH
73590.body:
Satoshi-era bitcoin whale moves over $200 million in BTC to FalconX, Cumberland | Tether plans GELT stablecoin launch with support from Georgian government | Indonesia blocks Polymarket amid crackdown on online betting | 'Institutional bid hasn't disappeared': Analysts say bitcoin cooldown, spot ETF outflows signal rotation, not exit |

The Daily ( https://www.theblock.co/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Written by James Hunt | Edited by Naga Avan-Nomayo

Presented by

LMAX ( https://www.theblock.co/lmax?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Happy Monday! It may be a holiday across the U.S. and in several other countries, but we're still here to keep you updated on the industry that never sleeps.

In today's newsletter, *Vitalik Buterin* says the *Ethereum Foundation* will sell less *ETH* , a *Satoshi* -era *bitcoin* whale moves over $200 million in *BTC* , *Tether* plans to launch a *GELT* stablecoin with *Georgian* government support, and more.

Meanwhile, a newly introduced ( https://www.theblock.co/post/402264/new-strategic-bitcoin-reserve-bill-drops-btc-purchase-target-adds-lockup?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) *strategic bitcoin reserve* bill dropped its 1 million BTC purchase target and added a 20-year lockup period.

*P.S.* Don't forget to check out The Funding , a biweekly rundown of crypto VC trends. It's a great read — and just like The Daily , it's free to subscribe ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters?select=The%20Funding&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) !

*THE BIG ONE*

*👀 Vitalik Buterin says Ethereum Foundation will be a 'smaller ship,' sell less ETH amid researcher exodus*

( https://www.theblock.co/post/402435/vitalik-buterin-says-ethereum-foundation-will-be-a-smaller-ship-sell-less-eth-amid-researcher-exodus?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

*Vitalik Buterin* said the *Ethereum Foundation* plans to become a "smaller ship" focused on longevity and *Ethereum's* censorship resistance, privacy, and security properties rather than broader ecosystem expansion.

* Buterin framed the post as his personal view rather than an official Ethereum Foundation position while defending the nonprofit's restructuring amid a wave of high-profile departures.
* The Ethereum co-founder added that the EF will sell less *ETH* going forward and emphasized the organization now controls just roughly 0.16% of the circulating *ether* supply.
* Buterin outlined three technical priorities for Ethereum's next phase: AI-assisted formal verification, resilient consensus design, and minimizing intermediaries across the network stack.
* He rejecte

73274.subject: Svadobná výzdoba
73274.body:
Zdravíme nevestu💍🤍,

Ďakujeme veľmi pekne za vašu správu.. Termín svadby máme ešte voľný a veľmi
radi budeme súčasťou vašej svadby.🤍

Spravidla sa s klientami stretávame osobne v showroome v Rozhanovciach
(kúsok od Košíc) kde si spolu vieme vyskladať svadobnú výzdobu a na základe
toho vytvoriť cenovú ponuku na mieru pre vás ak by ste mali záujem prosím
navrhnite deň a čas kedy by vám to najviac vyhovovalo aby sa časovo radi
prispôsobíme vášmu času. Poprípade, ak sme mimo Košíc či Prešova, radi si s
Vami dohodneme videohovor. Prosím navrhnite deň a čas, ktorý by Vám
vyhovoval najviac, pokojne aj cez víkend aj vo večerných hodinách. 🙂
Najbližsie voľne termíny máme ešte aj tento týždeň. Forme stretnutia sa
radi prispôsobíme Vám, čo je pre Vás praktickejšie ☺️

Budeme sa tešiť na prípadnú spoluprácu s Vami.

S pozdravom

Lenka Gattiker,
Vaša osobná posh asistentka

73231.subject: From a Cornell Lab to BlackRock. The Avalanche Story. | One Block Weekly #11
73231.body:
Hello,

Welcome back to One Block Weekly. One coin, one story.

While most blockchains were still arguing about speed, one quietly went and built a different kind of network altogether.

🏔️ Avalanche (AVAX)

Emin Gun Sirer is a Cornell University computer science professor who had been researching distributed systems long before most people knew what a blockchain was.

In 2018, his team published a whitepaper introducing a new consensus mechanism, a way for a network to agree on what is true, that was faster and more scalable than anything Bitcoin or Ethereum used. They called it Avalanche.

Ava Labs was founded to build a blockchain around it. Avalanche launched its mainnet in September 2020, raised $230 million in a public token sale in 48 hours, and immediately positioned itself as the platform serious institutions would eventually choose.

The pitch was simple: all the programmability of Ethereum, none of the congestion.

⚡ A Few Moments That Shaped Avalanche

2021 - AVAX surged over 2,700% in a single year, hitting an all-time high of around $147. Developers flooded in, and Avalanche briefly became the fourth largest blockchain by total value locked.

2022 - Avalanche partnered with Deloitte to build a disaster relief reimbursement platform for US government agencies. A blockchain landing a Big Four accounting firm and a government contract in the same deal was a first.

2023 - The subnet model gained traction. Major institutions, including financial firms and gaming companies, began launching their own custom blockchains, called subnets, built on top of Avalanche infrastructure.

2024 - BlackRock and other asset managers began tokenising real-world assets on Avalanche, bringing government bonds and funds onto the blockchain. Institutional adoption had moved from talk to action.

🔢 What Makes It Different

Avalanche finalises transactions in under two seconds, faster than almost any major blockchain. But the more interesting feature is subnets: custom blockchains that can be spun up within the Avalanche ecosystem, each with its own rules, validators, and token economics.

For institutions that want the benefits of blockchain without sharing space with memecoins and DeFi protocols, that is a compelling offer.

🤯 Three Weird Avalanche Facts

The founder once publicly exposed a flaw in a rival blockchain. Emin Gun Sirer identified and disclosed a critical vulnerability in Bitcoin's network years before Avalanche existed. He is one of the few people in crypto who arrived with an academic reputation already intact.

A major gaming subnet launched and went quiet. One of the highest profile early subnets was built for a blockchain gaming ecosystem. It launched with significant fanfare and struggled to find its audience, becoming an early lesson in the gap between institutional infrastructure and consumer adoption.

The whitepaper was published anonymously. The original Avalanche consensus paper was released und
